title: Understanding the Position Function
text: The position function is given as $$r(t)=3t^2 i-7t^3 j-5t^{\left(-2\right)} k$$. To find the speed, we need to first find the velocity by differentiating the position function with respect to time.

title: Differentiating the Position Function
text: Differentiate each component of the position function with respect to time $$t$$. Remember that the derivative of $$t^n$$ is $$n t^{n-1}$$.

title: Calculate the Velocity Components
text:
Differentiate each component:
- For the i component: $$3t^2$$ becomes $$6t$$
- For the j component: $$-7t^3$$ becomes $$-21t^2$$
- For the k component: $$-5t^{\left(-2ight)}$$ becomes $$10t^{\left(-3ight)}$$

title: Finding the Speed
text: The speed is the magnitude of the velocity vector. Use the formula: speed $$=$$ $$\sqrt{{v_x}^2+{v_y}^2+{v_z}^2}$$.

title: Calculate the Speed at $$t=1.0$$ s
text:
Substitute $$t=1.0$$ into the velocity components and calculate the magnitude:
- $$v_x$$ $$=$$ $$6	imes1$$
- $$v_y$$ $$=$$ $$-21	imes1^2$$
- $$v_z$$ $$=$$ $$10	imes1^{\left(-3ight)}$$
Then, calculate the speed: $$\sqrt{6^2+{\left(-21ight)}^2+{10}^2}$$.
